$comment: Template for harness-repo-package-remediation/harness.config.json (gitignored — copy this file, don't edit it).
The LLM toggle, resolved as: defaults < this file < environment < flow param.
Default with no file and no env: provider 'mock' — offline, deterministic, no key.
provider: mock | anthropic | openai | claude-sdk | github-sdk.
mock/anthropic/openai are the vendored platform's own raw-fetch lane.
claude-sdk/github-sdk are the vendored SDKs at harness-repo-package-remediation/vendors/{claude-sdk,github-sdk}
(symlinks to a sibling checkout — run `npm install` in the SDK's own root first).
CREDENTIALS ARE NOT CONFIGURED HERE. Each SDK reads its own from the environment at
the seam (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN for claude-sdk; the Copilot SDK
resolves its own), so a key never lands in a config file, a flow, a log, or a commit.
A requested SDK that cannot be constructed ABORTS the run rather than silently
degrading to mock; set allowMockFallback (or HARNESS_ALLOW_MOCK_FALLBACK=1) to opt out.
$services_comment: Optional: the services the flow's health probe checks up front (plan run-health-and-errors-log).
Unset ⇒ the built-in default [docker, verdaccio, devpi]. Each entry: { id, kind, ..., remedy }.
kind is a CLOSED strategy set: docker | registry (lane: npm|pip, delegates to the registry
preflight) | http (origin) | port (host, port). remedy is the sentence a human acts on when
the service is down — printed verbatim by errors.logs and the CLI exit line.
A bare { "id": "docker" } (or the string "docker") inherits the built-in definition.
